task_completion.description: Agent logged into the OpenClinica application.

task_completion.visual_evidence: The Subject Matrix, View Subject, or Edit Subject page is visible, specifically showing the subject label 'DM-105' within the 'Phase II Diabetes Trial' (or DM-TRIAL-2024).

task_completion.description: Agent selected the 'New York Hub' site for the subject.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A site selection dropdown, radio button, or reassignment field shows 'New York Hub' or 'DM-NY-02' selected for subject DM-105.

task_completion.visual_evidence: A text box, audit note, or reason field contains the exact text 'Patient relocated to NY'.

task_completion.visual_evidence: A success confirmation message is displayed, or the subject record for 'DM-105' is shown updated with 'New York Hub' or 'DM-NY-02' as their current site.

integrity.description: Agent used the OpenClinica web interface to perform the task, not terminal commands or direct database queries.
integrity.visual_evidence: All actions are performed within the web browser GUI. No terminal windows, scripts, or SQL clients are used to update the database directly.

integrity.description: Agent did not alter the subject's demographic data or original identifier.
integrity.visual_evidence: The subject's Date of Birth (1982-05-15) and Gender (Male) remain unchanged in any edit screens, and the subject identifier remains 'DM-105'.

integrity.description: Agent reassigned the existing subject rather than creating a new subject record.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent edits the existing 'DM-105' record. There is no evidence of the agent using the 'Add Subject' form to create a duplicate entry.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Study Identifier: DM-TRIAL-2024. Subject Label: DM-105. Source Site: DM-BOS-01. Target Site: DM-NY-02. Original Demographics: DOB 1982-05-15, Male. The task requires reassigning the existing subject to the target site with the reason 'Patient relocated to NY', without altering demographics or creating a new subject.
